title: Profiles, capabilities, slash commands, and runtime prompt commands
purpose: Feature 19 (profiles/capabilities), 38 (skills/slash commands/profiles/prompt templates), 39 (session variables in prompts), and 40 (runtime system prompt commands) are all unimplemented. The Profile struct is the missing glue that configures what model, tools, hooks, rules, instructions, and capabilities an agent gets. Slash commands pre-process user input before it reaches the model. Runtime system prompt commands populate dynamic prompt sections from shell commands. This brief implements all of them.
task: Define Profile struct with model, reasoning_effort, tools, hooks, rules, system_instructions, settings, capabilities. Define Capability as a composable unit of tools + instructions + hooks. Implement from_profile() that constructs a LoopContext (from N-017) and configures the ToolRegistry from a Profile. Implement slash command pre-processing in the loop (detect /command prefix, expand to skill or tool invocation). Implement runtime prompt commands (shell commands registered as system sections that execute at prompt construction time). Thread reasoning_effort from Profile through to ProviderRequest.

requirements.title: Profile struct and serialization
requirements.spec: Profile SHALL configure all aspects of an agent's behavior: model, reasoning effort, tools, hooks, rules, system instructions, settings, and composable capabilities.
acceptance: Profile struct in crates/norn/src/profile.rs

Fields: name (String), model (String), reasoning_effort (Option<ReasoningEffort>), tools (Option<Vec<String>>), system_instructions (Vec<String>), capabilities (Vec<Capability>), settings (HashMap<String, Value>)
Profile derives Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
Loads from TOML or JSON file via Profile::from_file(path)
Test: round-trip serialize/deserialize a profile with all fields set

requirements.title: Capability composition
requirements.spec: Capabilities SHALL be composable units that contribute tools, system instructions, and hooks. A Profile's capabilities are merged to produce the final configuration.
acceptance: Capability struct: name (String), tools (Vec<String>), system_instructions (Vec<String>), disallowed_patterns (Vec<String>)
Profile::resolved_tools() merges Profile.tools with all Capability.tools, deduplicates
Profile::resolved_instructions() concatenates Profile.system_instructions with all Capability.system_instructions
Profile::resolved_disallowed() collects all Capability.disallowed_patterns
Test: profile with 2 capabilities, verify merged tools and instructions

requirements.title: from_profile builds LoopContext and configures registry
requirements.spec: from_profile SHALL construct a fully configured LoopContext and restrict the ToolRegistry based on the Profile.
acceptance: from_profile(profile, registry, rules, hooks) -> (LoopContext, ToolRegistry)
Calls registry.set_available(profile.resolved_tools()) to restrict tools
Constructs LoopContext with profile.resolved_instructions() as system sections
Sets reasoning_effort on the LoopContext for threading to ProviderRequest
If profile has rules, loads them into the RuleEngine
Test: from_profile with tools=['read','write'], verify only those tools available

requirements.title: Thread reasoning_effort to ProviderRequest
requirements.spec: reasoning_effort from the Profile SHALL be passed through to the ProviderRequest. Currently call_provider hardcodes it to None.
acceptance: LoopContext gains field: reasoning_effort (Option<ReasoningEffort>)
call_provider reads reasoning_effort from LoopContext
ProviderRequest.reasoning is set from the LoopContext value
Test: set reasoning_effort to Low in LoopContext, verify ProviderRequest has it

requirements.title: Slash command preprocessing
requirements.spec: User input starting with / SHALL be intercepted before reaching the model and expanded into a skill activation or tool invocation.
acceptance: SlashCommand struct: name (String), handler (SlashCommandHandler enum)
SlashCommandHandler enum: Skill { skill_name: String }, Tool { tool_name: String, args: Value }, Custom { handler: Box<dyn Fn> }
SlashCommandRegistry in loop/commands.rs
preprocess_input(input, registry) -> PreprocessResult enum { Passthrough(String), Expanded { messages: Vec<Message> } }
Called in run_agent_step before the user prompt is added to messages
/compact, /help, /status as built-in examples
Test: input '/review foo.rs' expands to skill activation for 'review' with arg 'foo.rs'

requirements.title: Runtime system prompt commands
requirements.spec: Profiles SHALL be able to register shell commands that run at prompt construction time and contribute dynamic sections to the system prompt.
acceptance: PromptCommand struct: name (String), command (String), cache_ttl (Option<Duration>)
Profile gains field: prompt_commands (Vec<PromptCommand>)
At each iteration start, LoopContext evaluates prompt_commands via tokio::process::Command
Command stdout becomes a system section (append_system_section)
Cache: if cache_ttl set and previous result is fresh, reuse without re-executing
Command timeout: 5s default, failure produces diagnostic warning and skips section
Test: prompt command 'echo Current dir: $(pwd)' appears in system instruction

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement profile persistence or a profile registry — profiles are constructed by the caller
SHALL NOT implement capability discovery from filesystem — capabilities are defined in profile files
SHALL NOT implement profile-based provider routing (choosing Claude vs OpenAI based on profile) — that is caller responsibility
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass
Profile serialization round-trips correctly — verified by test
Capability composition produces correct merged tool list — verified by test
reasoning_effort flows from Profile to ProviderRequest — verified by test
Slash command expands to skill activation — verified by test
